handicap_result.real_condition: Algeria draw, so Algeria +1 wins after adjustment

j_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: 18 RMB ticket; J leg is the only leg
j_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: Argentina won by exactly 2, while the selected leg required Jordan not to lose or Argentina by exactly 1.
j_group_leg_settlement.notes: The model top-score cluster already included 0-2; selecting only +2 win omitted the exact two-goal margin.

j_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The leg required Austria by exactly 1; actual match was a draw.
j_group_leg_settlement.notes: Pre-match model and market both warned that draw was a central risk.
This confirms the need to separate Argentina win from Argentina exact-margin buckets.

j_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The selected ordinary 1X2 leg excluded draw; actual match drew 3-3.
j_group_leg_settlement.notes: This was a tail branch, but the draw floor was too large to omit in a multi-leg ticket.

j_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R1 user stake 16 RMB; full ticket also had non-J legs
j_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: Argentina won by exactly 2, not by exactly 1 or less.
j_group_leg_settlement.notes: R1 treated +2 win as low-score resistance, but the model had a strong 0-2 margin cluster.

j_group_leg_settlement.leg: Algeria win or Austria win, excluding draw
j_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R2 user stake 16 RMB; full ticket also had non-J legs
j_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The selected double-outcome structure deliberately excluded the actual 3-3 draw.
j_group_leg_settlement.notes: This is the clearest J-group portfolio error: the highest-risk omitted state was the result.

j_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R3 6x4 exact-margin system; J leg contribution only
j_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The leg required Austria by exactly 1; actual was a draw.
j_group_leg_settlement.notes: In a 6x4 structure, this leg can be omitted by some combinations, so full R3 settlement is outside J-only scope.

j_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R3 6x4 exact-margin system; J leg contribution only
j_group_leg_settlement.notes: The exact-margin read was correct for 072.

j_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R4 user stake 12 RMB; exact-score ticket
j_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: Argentina won by two, but Jordan scored and Argentina reached three goals.
j_group_leg_settlement.notes: The margin thesis was close, but the exact-score conversion was too narrow and missed BTTS/over tail.

j_group_leg_settlement.stake_scope: R5 user stake 12 RMB; full ticket also had non-J legs
j_group_leg_settlement.settlement_reason: The selected ordinary 1X2 leg required Austria to win; actual match was 3-3.
j_group_leg_settlement.notes: Austria direction was plausible pre-match, but the draw floor and 3-3 total-goal tail were both under-protected.

071_excluding_draw.diagnosis: Draw exclusion was the key structural miss. It should be classified as draw_floor_error plus portfolio_structure_error.

071_austria_win_or_handicap_draw.diagnosis: Austria by exactly one was a narrow-margin branch; actual draw invalidated both Austria win and +1 draw.

072_jordan_plus2_win_or_draw.diagnosis: Argentina by exactly two was the correct settlement bucket. Low-score resistance legs that stopped at +2 win missed the central 0-2/2-goal margin corridor.

argentina_score_cluster.diagnosis: Argentina win direction and two-goal margin were captured by nearby model paths, but exact-score tickets missed because Jordan scored and Argentina reached three goals. Classify as model_tail_error plus bet_translation_error for narrow score tickets.

direction_hits: Jordan vs Argentina ordinary direction: Argentina win
direction_misses_or_partial: Algeria vs Austria was a draw; ordinary Algeria win or Austria win legs missed.
draw_floor_errors: 071 anti-draw structures failed directly on the 3-3 draw.
R2 removed the key draw state despite model draw near 33% and market draw warning.
tail_errors: 071 total-goal tail: model leaned under 2.5 at 55.7%, but actual 3-3 was a six-goal outlier.
072 exact-score tail: 1-3 sat outside Top5 despite Argentina two-goal margin being close to 0-2.
market_or_context_errors: Third-round late-game state produced a high-event 071 draw rather than the low-event draw/one-goal corridors.
Argentina rotation did not prevent a 3-goal output, while Jordan still scored.

bet_translation_errors: T1/R1 selected Jordan +2 win while model score cluster already contained 0-2, the actual +2 draw margin.
R4 converted Argentina low-score cluster into 0-1/0-2 only and missed 1-3.
portfolio_structure_errors: R2 anti-draw leg made the most important 071 risk fatal.
T4/R3 exact-margin chains were too dependent on narrow one-goal/two-goal branches.
T6/R5 tail tickets depended on ordinary wins while draw remained large.
hedge_independence: J legs that looked like protection were not independent if they omitted the exact draw or exact-margin bucket.
narrow_path_risk: Exact margin and exact score should remain separated from match direction and ordinary win probability.
Argentina win by two was a successful margin bucket, but exact 0-2 was not robust enough.

rule_updates.update: If model draw is at or above 30%, or market draw is materially higher than model, any leg that explicitly excludes draw must be capped as high variance and cannot be used as coverage.
For +2 and -1 markets, split win/draw/loss by exact real-score margin before portfolio construction; do not merge +2 win and +2 draw under a generic small-loss label.
When Top5 contains adjacent score corridors such as 0-1, 0-2, 0-3 and 1-2, exact-score tickets must document omitted BTTS and one-extra-goal tails.
Third-round simultaneous matches need a late-event tail layer; 071 shows low-event pre-match reads can become 3-3 when stoppage-time incentives and qualification math collide.
Report leg hit separately from ticket hit. T4 had the 072 exact-margin leg hit, but the ticket failed once 071 missed.
Post-result reviews may update settlement and rules only; they must not propose fresh purchase paths from observed outcomes.

final_verdict.summary: J-group betting-risk lesson is not that favorite or underdog directions were broadly wrong; the main failures were draw exclusion in 071, exact-margin overuse, and narrow score translation around Argentina's two-goal win.
